title: LoupeAI (ai.loupe.json)
description: The BYTES of the `ai` derivative blob the farm writes to /.juicemount/derivatives/<inode>/ai.loupe.json and registers via POST /derivatives/register (OL-1 / JM-17). This is a LoupeJSON document, not a control-plane response. OpenLoupe's D6 consume (applyLoupeJSON) ingests it as a DESTRUCTIVE REPLACE, so two invariants are load-bearing: (Rule A) every delivery is the COMPLETE current bundle — present what's computed, explicit null for the rest, never a per-kind partial; (Rule B) the `media` block carries the FULL tech every time — a sparse media block NULL-binds good tech columns. This schema enforces Rule B by requiring all 10 media fields with additionalProperties:false. Keys are explicit snake_case; embedding vectors are base64 of a packed little-endian Float32 array, L2-normalized; times are integer milliseconds. Authored from internal/farm/transcript.go (LoupeJSON Codable structs) and AI_DELIVERY_SPEC.md.

schema_version.description: Producer schema/algo version of the bundle, e.g. "1.0".

indexed_at.description: RFC3339 / ISO8601 timestamp the bundle was produced.

media.description: FULL tech block (Rule B). applyLoupeJSON refreshes the asset's width/height/duration_ms/fps/codec/color_space/log_format/hash_xxh3 columns from here and NULL-binds any MISSING field — so all 10 fields are REQUIRED. Genuinely-absent color_space/log_format are explicit null (still present). A sparse media block is a data-loss hazard and MUST fail this schema.

type.description: Top-level media class. Farm emits "video" (or "audio" for audio-only).

fps.description: Frames per second (may be fractional, e.g. 59.94).

color_space.description: Matrix coefficients / color space, e.g. "bt709", "bt2020nc". Explicit null when unknown (still present — Rule B).

log_format.description: Camera log curve, e.g. "slog3", "logc4". Explicit null for non-log (still present — Rule B).

hash_xxh3.description: xxh3 hex of the SOURCE bytes the AI was computed from. MUST equal the manifest's source_hash for this inode; OpenLoupe's D6 gate compares them and fails closed on mismatch.

ai.description: The AI sub-bundle. Optional at the envelope level (may be omitted/null before any AI lands), but when present every sub-kind is stated explicitly: present what's computed, null what isn't (Rule A — always the COMPLETE current set, never a per-kind regression).

image_embedding_global.description: One CLIP ViT-L/14 vector for the whole clip: base64 of a packed little-endian Float32[768], L2-normalized. null until embeddings land (OL-2).

scene_embeddings.description: Per-scene CLIP vectors. null until embeddings land.

t_ms.description: Scene timestamp in ms from clip start.

embedding.description: base64 Float32[768], L2-normalized (same space as image_embedding_global).

auto_tags.description: Discrete labels. Farm leaves null (CLIP/ImageNet labels won't match OpenLoupe's VNClassify vocabulary — contribute embeddings, not tags).

faces.description: Detected/clustered faces. null until faces land (OL-2: dlib/resnet-v1-128, dim 128).

cluster_id.description: Stable cluster identity for the face.

t_ms.description: Timestamp in ms of the detection frame.

transcript.description: ASR transcript. null when the clip has no audio / no speech (NEVER an empty segments:[] — omit/null instead). The farm's current target.

language.description: ISO-639-1 ("en") or whisper's detected lang code.

model.description: provider/model id, e.g. "whisper.cpp/large-v3". The invalidation key OpenLoupe stamps + shows for provenance.

segments.description: Ordered by start_ms, ascending, non-overlapping; phrase/sentence granularity (~5-15 words) for the karaoke Transcript Theater. Never empty (a transcript with no segments must be delivered as null, not []).

start_ms.description: Segment start in ms from clip start.

end_ms.description: Segment end in ms; start_ms <= end_ms.

ocr.description: On-screen text lines (Tesseract/PaddleOCR → ocr_text FTS). DELIBERATELY NOT the destructive-replace rule of the other AI kinds: omit/null ⇒ OpenLoupe leaves existing OCR alone; [] ⇒ clears OCR; non-empty ⇒ replaces. So if the farm does NOT do OCR for a clip, OMIT this key (do not send []).

t_ms.description: Timestamp in ms of the recognized line.

ai_provider_summary.description: Map of sub-kind → provider/model id (e.g. {"transcript":"whisper.cpp/large-v3","embeddings":"OpenCLIP/ViT-L-14/LAION-2B"}) for provenance.
